note: Rule summaries are independent restatements written for this project, not reproductions of BARR-C:2018 text. BARR-C:2018 is copyright Barr Group (Integrated Embedded, LLC); its rule numbering is used here to identify the guidelines, as the standard invites tools to do. Consult the official document at barrgroup.com for the normative wording.

1.1a.summary: A build that emits no warnings at the compiler's most aggressive setting, so real defects are never lost in routine noise.
1.1a.fix: Enable -Wall -Wextra -Werror (or equivalent) and eliminate every warning.

1.3a.summary: Braces around every control-statement body, including one-line and empty ones, so adding a second statement cannot silently change scope.
1.3a.fix: Add braces to every controlled block, even single statements.

1.7a.summary: Unstructured jumps are avoided: goto in particular, and any exit that leaves a reader unable to follow control flow by reading downward.
1.7a.fix: Restructure control flow with loops and status flags.

1.7c.summary: A condition tests a value; it does not perform an assignment whose result is then tested, which is how = gets written for ==.
1.7c.fix: Assign first, compare on the next line.

2.1a.summary: Comment syntax stays within the two standard forms, and comment openers are never embedded inside a comment where they do not nest.
2.1a.fix: Normalize comment style and remove nesting.

2.2.summary: Disabled code is deleted rather than commented out, since version control already remembers it and stale commented blocks mislead readers.
2.2.fix: Delete dead code blocks living in comments.

3.1a.summary: A bounded line width, so side-by-side review and diff tools show a full line without wrapping or horizontal scrolling.
3.1a.fix: Wrap long lines at logical boundaries.

3.2a.summary: Indentation uses spaces alone, so the alignment a reader sees does not depend on how their editor happens to render a tab.
3.2a.fix: Convert tabs to spaces per the project indent width.

4.2a.summary: A module presents its callers a header describing only what they may use, keeping internal structure out of the public interface.
4.2a.fix: Split public API into a header; keep internals static in the .c file.

5.2a.summary: Numeric data uses the width-explicit types from <stdint.h>, because the built-in types vary in size between targets and toolchains.
5.2a.fix: Replace basic types with int8_t..uint64_t as appropriate.

5.4a.summary: Literal suffixes are written in upper case, since a lower-case l is easily misread as the digit one in most fonts.

5.6a.summary: Firmware avoids the heap once running, so worst-case memory use stays provable and allocation cannot fail at an inconvenient moment.
5.6a.fix: Allocate statically or from init-time pools only.

6.1a.summary: Call graphs stay acyclic, direct and mutual recursion alike, so worst-case stack depth can be bounded by inspection.
6.1a.fix: Rewrite recursive algorithms iteratively with bounded storage.

6.2a.summary: Anything not part of a module's published interface is given internal linkage, keeping the global namespace to what is genuinely shared.

6.3a.summary: Functions stay short enough to read in one sitting -- on the order of a printed page -- so their behaviour can be reasoned about whole.

7.1e.summary: Identifiers are distinguishable by more than letter case, and leave the leading-underscore namespace to the implementation that reserves it.
7.1e.fix: Rename identifiers to unambiguous, non-reserved names.

8.2a.summary: A condition states the comparison it means, rather than relying on a value being incidentally non-zero.

8.3a.summary: A pointer is tested against NULL explicitly, so the reader sees a pointer check rather than something that could be an integer test.

8.5a.summary: Every switch carries a default clause, giving unexpected values a defined destination instead of falling through the statement silently.
8.5a.fix: Add a default branch with error handling or an assertion.

8.6a.summary: Loop termination is provable, so an unbounded loop is a deliberate, documented decision rather than an oversight.
8.6a.fix: Bound loops with counters/timeouts; document intentional scheduler loops.
